hK-7cHa69lk.title: Bemba Greetings
hK-7cHa69lk.prompt_used: This is a Bemba language lesson. The instructor teaches Bemba greetings, the alphabet, and counting. Bemba greetings: mulishani (how are you), mwabombeni, eya (yes), awe (no), natotela (thank you), sh...

segments.text: Hi, welcome to Bembling. You are going to be learning greetings in Bemba.
I am fine, thanks. How are you, with respect?
Now, notice that these two words are said the same.
Good afternoon and good evening, but nonetheless you can still use them.
Have a nice day or have a nice resting day.
Thank you for going through Lesson 1 and hopefully you will tag along even to the other lessons.

hK-7cHa69lk.full_text: Hi, welcome to Bembling. You are going to be learning greetings in Bemba. So, let's get going. Hello. Hi. Mulishani. Welcome. Mulishani. Welcome, as with respect. Mulishani mukwe. I am fine, thanks. How are you, with respect? Mulishani. How are you, with respect? Mulishani. Good morning. How are you? Good afternoon. Chunguronkwe. Good evening. Chunguronkwe. Now, notice that these two words are said the same. Good afternoon and good evening, but nonetheless you can still use them. Good night. And bye. Sendaminipongkwe. Goodbye. Shalinipo. Shalinipo. Stay in peace. Mushale umutende. Have a nice day or have a nice resting day. Mutushe wino. Work well. Uwombe wino. See you later. Tukamonandalimbi. Thank you for going through Lesson 1 and hopefully you will tag along even to the other lessons. Thank you.

itRWkSBaPm8.title: Bemba Alphabet System and Pronunciations Lesson 2
itRWkSBaPm8.prompt_used: This is a Bemba language lesson. The instructor teaches Bemba greetings, the alphabet, and counting. Bemba greetings: mulishani (how are you), mwabombeni, eya (yes), awe (no), natotela (thank you), sh...

segments.text: Hello, Hi. Welcome to Bembling Lesson 2. In this lesson, we are going to cover the Alphabet
System. Learning to read and pronounce Bemba words.
The topics to be covered are vowels, consonants, and syllables. Vowels. A, E, I, O, U. You
could try that out yourself. Consonants. There are 21 consonants in the alphabet system.
Notice that there are some that have been omitted. That mentioned Q, R, V, X, and Z
have been omitted because they are not commonly used. We will now look at syllables. Learning
We have taken the following example. Did you get that right? As in prayer. Let us try
out some more examples in order to become more confident. Iwe. As in you in English.
As in afraid. Feeling cold. To hide. To come. I hope you got those examples right. So, see you in Lesson 3.
And if you have any questions, you can comment them in the comment section. Thank you.

itRWkSBaPm8.full_text: Hello, Hi. Welcome to Bembling Lesson 2. In this lesson, we are going to cover the Alphabet System. Learning to read and pronounce Bemba words. The topics to be covered are vowels, consonants, and syllables. Vowels. A, E, I, O, U. You could try that out yourself. Consonants. There are 21 consonants in the alphabet system. Notice that there are some that have been omitted. That mentioned Q, R, V, X, and Z have been omitted because they are not commonly used. We will now look at syllables. Learning We have taken the following example. Did you get that right? As in prayer. Let us try out some more examples in order to become more confident. Iwe. As in you in English. As in afraid. Feeling cold. To hide. To come. I hope you got those examples right. So, see you in Lesson 3. And if you have any questions, you can comment them in the comment section. Thank you.

gpMyp5EkJ0k.title: Bemba Numbers and Counting Lesson 3
gpMyp5EkJ0k.prompt_used: This is a Bemba language lesson. The instructor teaches Bemba greetings, the alphabet, and counting. Bemba greetings: mulishani (how are you), mwabombeni, eya (yes), awe (no), natotela (thank you), sh...

segments.text: Hello, hi. Welcome to Lesson 3 of Bembling. In this lesson, we are going to be looking
at numbers. So, let's get going.
Number one, cimo, cimo. Number two, fibili, fibili. Number three, fitatu, fitatu. Number
four, fine, fine. Number five, fisano, fisano. Number six, mutanda, mutanda. And number seven,
cine lubali, cine lubali. Number eight, cine konse, konse. Number nine, pabula, pabula.
Number ten, ikumi, ikumi. Thank you for listening through.
